metadata.title: AlphaVest WealthOS Contextual User Manual V3
metadata.boundary: Generated manual production artifact, not a production certification.

tasks.title: Configure the platform policy baseline
tasks.outcome: Platform settings, advice-boundary rules, role templates, security defaults, evidence templates, and export defaults are visible and ready for tenant setup.
tasks.result: Policy baseline is reviewed; sensitive changes require confirmation and audit.
tasks.sourceStatus: E3 navigable UI with visible policy states; broad persisted policy transactions are not claimed.
tasks.whyThisTaskExists: AlphaVest needs a policy baseline before client work starts because advice boundaries, role templates, evidence expectations, security defaults, and export rules define what later workflows may safely do. Without this baseline, teams would be moving client data and review work through unclear controls.
tasks.whatTheUserIsTryingToAchieve: The user is trying to confirm that the operating model is safe enough for tenant setup and that sensitive changes still require review, confirmation, and audit.
whatThisEnablesNext: tenant setup with consistent policy inheritance
clear role templates for invitations and governance
future evidence templates for important actions

steps.userAction: Open start screen and verify role/tenant context
steps.whyItMatters: Opening the entry screen confirms that the user is working in the intended task context before acting.
downstreamDependency: tenant setup with consistent policy inheritance
clear role templates for invitations and governance

gates.rationale: Second confirmation protects sensitive platform, security, and role changes from accidental activation.
Advice-boundary policy protects clients from seeing advice-like material before human review and compliance release.
Audit expectations preserve the lineage of sensitive configuration changes.

blockedStates.controlRationale: This state confirms the intended demo path while preserving the rule that gates cannot be bypassed.
blockedStates.userCanDo: Review, select, enter, or confirm permitted data.
blockedStates.userCannotDo: Bypass required gates or tenant/role scope.
blockedStates.nextLegitimateAction: Policy baseline is reviewed; sensitive changes require confirmation and audit.

blockedStates.controlRationale: This state protects the workflow from being forced forward when role, evidence, compliance, data, or implementation conditions are not satisfied.
blockedStates.userCanDo: Review the reason, request evidence, change role, or return to the correct prior step.
blockedStates.userCannotDo: Force completion or claim persistence when the app only shows a static/demo state.
blockedStates.nextLegitimateAction: Resolve the missing role, evidence, compliance, or data requirement.

tasks.demoBoundaryNote: In the current demo, the UI shows policy states and confirmation patterns. It does not prove broad persisted policy transactions across every platform setting.
tasks.futureReadinessNote: This prepares the workflow for production governance because later tenant policies, release checks, audit history, and evidence templates can inherit one controlled baseline instead of relying on ad hoc screen behavior.

tasks.title: Create and prepare a client tenant
tasks.outcome: A tenant moves from list/create/setup into team assignment, policy selection, and user invitation readiness.
tasks.result: Tenant setup checklist shows whether activation gates are ready or blocked.
tasks.sourceStatus: E3 navigable UI; tenant write workflow is a required input-mask slice, not yet fully governed.
tasks.whyThisTaskExists: A client tenant is the container that keeps family-office data, roles, policies, evidence, and workflow state isolated from every other client. Creating it carefully prevents later work from being attached to the wrong client, jurisdiction, or service team.
tasks.whatTheUserIsTryingToAchieve: The user is trying to prepare a client workspace that has the right identity, jurisdiction, service tier, assigned team, policy profile, and invitation path.
whatThisEnablesNext: client profile and document intake under the correct tenant

tasks.futureReadinessNote: This prepares the workflow for production tenant isolation, onboarding automation, team assignment reporting, and policy inheritance once governed persistence is expanded.

tasks.title: Accept an invitation and complete onboarding
tasks.outcome: The invited user accepts the invitation, confirms identity/MFA, accepts privacy/consent, and acknowledges role scope.
tasks.result: User reaches role confirmation; production auth remains intentionally deferred.
tasks.sourceStatus: E2/E3 visual and navigable onboarding surfaces; no real authentication claim.
tasks.whyThisTaskExists: Onboarding connects a person to the right tenant, role, consent version, and access scope. This matters because later decisions, evidence views, and communications should depend on who the user is allowed to be in that client context.
tasks.whatTheUserIsTryingToAchieve: The user is trying to confirm that the invitation belongs to the expected tenant, acknowledge required privacy context, and understand the role scope they will operate under.
whatThisEnablesNext: governed communication and consent history in later production flows

tasks.demoBoundaryNote: In the current demo, onboarding screens are visual and navigable. They do not introduce real production authentication.
tasks.futureReadinessNote: This prepares the workflow for provider-backed authentication, consent versioning, and role-scoped client access without changing the demo-first implementation order.

tasks.title: Submit client profile and family context
tasks.outcome: Client profile, family members, and relationships provide context for later decisions and access.
tasks.result: Profile and relationship context is visible with conflicts or missing evidence flagged.
tasks.sourceStatus: E3 navigable UI with static/demo data; profile persistence is a P0/P1 implementation need.
tasks.whyThisTaskExists: Family-office work depends on relationships, responsibilities, objectives, and governance preferences. Capturing that context helps AlphaVest distinguish useful information from incomplete or conflicted inputs.
tasks.whatTheUserIsTryingToAchieve: The user is trying to make the client profile clear enough that later reviewers can understand family roles, decision scopes, relationship conflicts, and missing evidence.
whatThisEnablesNext: better evidence requests and analyst follow-up

tasks.futureReadinessNote: This prepares the workflow for richer family governance, permission scoping, conflict review, and future data-quality automation.

tasks.title: Create an entity and review wealth structure
tasks.outcome: Entity records, participants, documents, wealth-map nodes, and action cards expose structure gaps safely.
tasks.result: Entity detail, wealth-map drawer, and action-board blockers show what must happen next.
tasks.sourceStatus: E3 navigable UI; entity/action mutations and evidence gate need transaction wiring.
tasks.whyThisTaskExists: Wealth operations need a clear map of entities, participants, jurisdictions, assets, and missing proof. Entity intake turns loose structure information into nodes that can be reviewed, evidenced, and acted on.
tasks.whatTheUserIsTryingToAchieve: The user is trying to create or review a structure node and understand what evidence or action is still needed before the structure can support decisions.
whatThisEnablesNext: evidence-backed review of ownership and participants

tasks.futureReadinessNote: This prepares the workflow for richer wealth-map analysis, specialist review, automated gap detection, and evidence-backed entity governance.

tasks.title: Upload and verify a document
tasks.role: Client / Family CFO / External Advisor / Analyst
tasks.outcome: A document is uploaded, extraction is reviewed as draft, and human verification or evidence linking is required.
tasks.result: Document reaches verification-pending or needs-clarification state.
tasks.sourceStatus: E3 navigable UI; no real file storage/extraction transaction is claimed.
tasks.whyThisTaskExists: Documents are the source proof behind profile, entity, recommendation, export, and compliance decisions. Uploading and verifying them reduces reliance on memory, assumptions, or unreviewed extracted data.
tasks.whatTheUserIsTryingToAchieve: The user is trying to upload source evidence, classify it, review extracted draft data, correct uncertainty, and move it toward human verification.
whatThisEnablesNext: future data extraction and document-quality workflows

tasks.futureReadinessNote: This prepares the workflow for production file storage, extraction confidence handling, analyst review queues, evidence automation, and redacted export packaging.

tasks.title: Process a signal and route internal work
tasks.outcome: The analyst reviews an internal-only trigger, requests data or routes to advisor, and keeps client visibility blocked.
tasks.result: Trigger moves toward awaiting information or advisor review while staying internal-only.
tasks.sourceStatus: E4 demo-state executable for J01 subset; not fully governed across all signals.
tasks.whyThisTaskExists: Signals may indicate missing ownership data, funding questions, document gaps, or workflow triggers. Analyst review separates internal triage from client-visible recommendations.
tasks.whatTheUserIsTryingToAchieve: The user is trying to understand the signal, add internal context, and choose the next safe route without creating client-visible advice.
whatThisEnablesNext: audit-backed triage for the J01 subset

tasks.demoBoundaryNote: In the current demo, the J01 signal/advisor subset has the strongest executable proof. Not every signal path is fully governed.
tasks.futureReadinessNote: This prepares the workflow for broader signal governance, triage metrics, automated queueing, and safer recommendation preparation.

tasks.title: Review an advisor approval package
tasks.outcome: Advisor approves, revises, requests more data, or escalates without releasing to the client.
tasks.result: Advisor decision is recorded or routed; compliance release remains required.
tasks.sourceStatus: E4 for J01 advisor action; advisor approval is explicitly not client visibility.
tasks.whyThisTaskExists: Advisor review is the human judgment stage between analyst work and compliance release. It lets advisors approve, revise, request more data, or escalate without granting client visibility.
tasks.whatTheUserIsTryingToAchieve: The user is trying to review the recommendation package, record a defensible advisor decision, and route the work to the next gate.
whatThisEnablesNext: escalation to calls when complexity needs human conversation

tasks.futureReadinessNote: This prepares the workflow for clearer advisor accountability, package quality metrics, and compliance-ready recommendation records.

tasks.title: Release, block, or request evidence for advice-like content
tasks.outcome: Compliance determines whether content can become client-visible, stays blocked, or needs more evidence.
tasks.result: Release, block, or evidence-request state is visible; no unapproved advice reaches the client.
tasks.sourceStatus: E3/E4 mixed demo states; broad release transaction is still a top implementation gap.
tasks.whyThisTaskExists: Compliance release is the decisive client-visibility gate. It checks whether classification, evidence, permissions, disclosures, and review lineage are strong enough for the client to see the material.
tasks.whatTheUserIsTryingToAchieve: The user is trying to release, block, or request evidence in a way that preserves the no-unapproved-advice rule and leaves a defensible record.
whatThisEnablesNext: client-visible content only after gates pass

tasks.title: Review and submit a released client decision
tasks.outcome: Client reviews only released content, chooses accept/defer/reject, and receives proof of submission.
tasks.result: Decision success page and linked evidence record show the completion proof.
tasks.sourceStatus: E3 navigable UI with demo workflow actions; final decision/evidence package persistence remains incomplete.
tasks.whyThisTaskExists: Client decisions should be based only on material that has passed the release gate. The decision workflow records acceptance, deferral, rejection, or a request for more information with evidence context.
tasks.whatTheUserIsTryingToAchieve: The user is trying to understand the released decision context and record a response that can be traced later.

tasks.futureReadinessNote: This prepares the workflow for richer family approval chains, review scheduling, evidence-backed decision history, and client-facing status clarity.

tasks.title: Review evidence and create a controlled export
tasks.outcome: Evidence is inspected and exported only through scoped, redacted, approved, audited export steps.
tasks.result: Export package is previewed/downloaded only if scope, redaction, permission, and approval allow it.
tasks.sourceStatus: E3 visual/navigable; export package/file/share realism is a Phase 18 gap.
tasks.whyThisTaskExists: Evidence and export workflows make proof portable without making all underlying data visible. They help users share the right subset of records while preserving redaction, permission, expiry, and audit controls.
tasks.whatTheUserIsTryingToAchieve: The user is trying to find relevant evidence, choose an export scope, apply redaction, confirm approval, and avoid exposing restricted material.

blockedStates.nextLegitimateAction: Export package is previewed/downloaded only if scope, redaction, permission, and approval allow it.

blockedStates.controlRationale: This state protects the workflow from being forced forward when role, evidence, compliance, data, or implementation conditions are not satisfied.
blockedStates.userCanDo: Review the reason, request evidence, change role, or return to the correct prior step.
blockedStates.userCannotDo: Force completion or claim persistence when the app only shows a static/demo state.
blockedStates.nextLegitimateAction: Resolve the missing role, evidence, compliance, or data requirement.

tasks.demoBoundaryNote: In the current demo, evidence and export screens are visual and navigable. Export package generation, file realism, and share-link behavior remain future implementation work.
tasks.futureReadinessNote: This prepares the workflow for production-grade data rooms, evidence packages, activity-log exports, privacy review, and auditable disclosure history.

tasks.title: Manage governance users, roles, and access requests
tasks.outcome: Sensitive user/role/access changes require scoped roles, confirmation, and audit history.
tasks.result: Access request is approved/denied/escalated and audit history shows lineage.
tasks.sourceStatus: E3/E4 demo actions visible; governed role/access transactions remain incomplete.
tasks.whyThisTaskExists: Role and permission changes can expose documents, decisions, evidence, exports, and governance controls. AlphaVest needs access changes to be scoped, confirmed, reviewable, and auditable.
tasks.whatTheUserIsTryingToAchieve: The user is trying to assign, revoke, approve, deny, or escalate access in a way that preserves tenant scope and audit lineage.
whatThisEnablesNext: future permission governance and security reporting

tasks.demoBoundaryNote: In the current demo, governance screens and selected demo actions are visible. Fully governed role and access transactions remain incomplete.
tasks.futureReadinessNote: This prepares the workflow for production access reviews, separation-of-duties controls, delegated administration, and security audit reporting.

tasks.title: Choose a communication or escalation path
tasks.outcome: The right secure message, data request, call, workshop, external specialist, or compliance escalation path is selected.
tasks.result: Communication path or call-trigger state is visible and linked to evidence when material.
tasks.sourceStatus: E2/E3 mostly static communication surfaces; message/call persistence not claimed.
tasks.whyThisTaskExists: Not every issue belongs in the same channel. AlphaVest needs a way to distinguish secure messages, data requests, advisor calls, workshops, external specialist handoffs, and compliance escalations.
tasks.whatTheUserIsTryingToAchieve: The user is trying to choose the least risky communication path that can resolve the issue while preserving evidence and compliance context where material.

tasks.title: Monitor operations, SLA, and reference state
tasks.outcome: Ops reviews queues, SLA risk, service blueprint, roadmap, and state catalogue for delivery/control monitoring.
tasks.result: Bottlenecks, SLA breaches, roadmap scope, and state language are visible for internal use.
tasks.sourceStatus: E2/E3 reference and dashboard surfaces; queue/SLA mutation not yet implemented.
tasks.whyThisTaskExists: Operations users need to see bottlenecks, SLA pressure, roadmap boundaries, and state definitions so the service model can improve without confusing reference material with client workflow.
tasks.whatTheUserIsTryingToAchieve: The user is trying to understand current workload, risk, reference state, and product scope without treating reference pages as client task flows.

fields.userMeaning: Classify information, workflow, guidance, advice-relevant, or advice
fields.validation: Cannot weaken release rule without reason

fields.userMeaning: Create tenant identity and regulatory context
fields.validation: Required and unique enough for demo
All required boxes must be selected

fields.exampleDemoValue: Morgan Family Office / Family Office Advisory
fields.validation: No final advice fields; required basics

fields.userMeaning: Define family role and decision scope
fields.allowedValues: Principal, CFO, Trustee, Beneficiary, Next Gen
Allowed file types and document taxonomy
fields.validation: Unsupported file or missing scope blocks upload

fields.userMeaning: Confirm or correct AI draft data
Approve, revise, request data, or escalate
fields.allowedValues: Approve, Revise, Request More Data, Escalate
fields.validation: Approval does not release to client

fields.userMeaning: Accept, defer, reject, or request more information
Choose secure message or escalation path

qaExpectations: The manual is UI-task-led rather than route-path-led.
Every task chapter includes why, goal, next-use, screenshot, procedure, fields, gates, blocked states, future readiness, and demo boundary.
The PDF is text-extractable and visually rendered to PNG for inspection.
